narrative.headline: What 2015 Ford Explorer owners report most
narrative.summary: Structural concerns make up the largest share of owner complaints for this Explorer model year, followed closely by steering issues. Engine complaints include a notable pattern of exhaust fume intrusion into the cabin, while back-over prevention systems also draw reports. Across all four areas, owners describe discovering problems early in ownership, often within the first weeks or months of driving a new vehicle.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: Owners report dissatisfaction with structural integrity shortly after taking delivery, with complaints surfacing within the first few months and low miles of ownership. · 4 crash · 4 injured

issues.title: Steering
issues.blurb: Owners report purchasing vehicles with steering they describe as damaged or defective from the start, with dealers allegedly declining to repair the condition. · 4 crash · 10 injured

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report detecting exhaust fumes and carbon monoxide inside the cabin of new vehicles, with the odor present almost immediately after purchase. · 1 fire · 1 crash · 7 injured

issues.title: Back Over Prevention
issues.blurb: Owners report that the backup camera and sensors operate inconsistently, with the camera image sometimes appearing, sometimes absent, and the touchscreen displaying prompts to contact the dealership. · 1 crash
